opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The condom broke last night, my tracking app says I'm ovulating today, and I am absolutely terrified I'm going to get pregnant. I need the morning-after pill.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It happened about 12 hours ago and I want to do whatever gives me the best chance of not getting pregnant, as fast as possible.

symptom_narrative.onset: the condom broke during sex last night, about 12 hours ago
symptom_narrative.character: no physical symptoms — mostly panic and worry about becoming pregnant
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: emotionally it's the worst — I can't stop thinking about it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing yet — I want to get the morning-after pill
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: my app saying I'm ovulating today makes it feel worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no nausea, no vomiting, no tummy or pelvic pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never had to use emergency contraception before

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: adamant about avoiding pregnancy right now for personal and work reasons; tracks her cycle with a phone app and says she is on day 14 of a regular 28-day cycle

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, anxious questions about how effective the pill is and how quickly she needs it

objective_data_offered.value: phone tracking app says day 14 of a regular 28-day cycle and that she is ovulating today
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: app-predicted ovulation is an estimate, not a measured or confirmed ovulation date

objective_data_offered.value: last normal period was about 14 days ago
